name: C.S. Lewis
title: Author & Apologist
bio: British writer and lay theologian, author of 'The Chronicles of Narnia,' 'Mere Christianity,' and 'The Screwtape Letters.' Fellow of Magdalen College, Oxford and later Professor at Cambridge.
faith: A former atheist who converted to Christianity in 1931, Lewis became one of the most influential Christian apologists of the 20th century. He described his conversion in 'Surprised by Joy' and defended the faith in works like 'Mere Christianity.'

tagline: The most reluctant convert who became Christianity's greatest modern apologist
